tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Young Man's Sickle Cell 'Functional Cure' Becomes Mission to Free Others
stories.summary: Daniel Cressy, 23, became Louisiana's first person functionally cured of sickle cell through gene therapy and now leads a nonprofit so others won't feel the loneliness he endured.

stories.headline: Mother's Life Jacket Charity Saves Three Teens in Water Rescue
stories.summary: Article excerpt provided contains no information about the mother's life jacket charity or water rescue—unable to rewrite summary.

stories.headline: Woman Builds 'Wind Phone' to Help Grieving Hearts Connect With Lost Loved Ones
stories.summary: Article excerpt provided contains no information about the wind phone or grieving woman—unable to rewrite summary.

stories.headline: Endangered Sea Turtles Return to Restored Philippine Sanctuary
stories.summary: Marine turtles nested twice in just 16 days along Carmen's protected coastline, proving years of habitat restoration work is paying off.

stories.headline: World's First Huntington's Stem Cell Trial Shows Promise With No Serious Harm
stories.summary: UCI Health launched the world's first embryonic stem cell trial for Huntington's disease, with hNSC-01 cells implanted directly into patients' brains during six-hour MRI surgeries.

stories.headline: High School Friends' Anonymous Compliments Account Hits 7 Million Views
stories.summary: Zac Staskiewicz and Lily Duea-Ebner, two choir friends, created @boro_compliments where Hillsborough High students anonymously uplifted each other, reaching 7 million views.

stories.headline: Leatherback Sea Turtles Reach Record Nesting Numbers at Cape Hatteras
stories.summary: Four endangered leatherback sea turtles nested at Cape Hatteras in 2026—the highest count since monitoring began in the 1980s, marking a remarkable ocean comeback.

stories.headline: Oklahoma Baseball Captures First National Title in 30 Years
stories.summary: Article excerpt provided contains no information about Oklahoma baseball's championship—unable to rewrite summary.

stories.headline: Brazilian Engineer Transforms Bacteria Into Clean Energy Power Plants
stories.summary: Bioprocess engineer Giulia Castro harnessed Synechocystis pevalekii bacteria to generate 227 mW per square meter while capturing CO₂ and releasing oxygen.

stories.headline: Rare New Zealand Parakeets Thrive as Rats Vanish From Their Valley
stories.summary: New Zealand rangers declared zero rats in Hawdon valley, where critically endangered orange-fronted parakeets now have 22 active nests—up from zero five years ago.

stories.headline: Cardinals Fans Give Nolan Arenado a Tearful Welcome Home
stories.summary: Nolan Arenado received a lengthy standing ovation from St. Louis Cardinals fans when he returned to Busch Stadium wearing Arizona colors for the first time.

stories.headline: Thought-Extinct Ocean Species Found Thriving Off California Coast
stories.summary: Scientists discovered 18 sunflower sea stars—each spanning over three feet—thriving off Northern California after the species was thought lost to devastating disease.

stories.headline: Contractor Sees News Story, Shows Up to Help Struggling Couple for Free
stories.summary: A Virginia contractor spotted a news story about a Chesterfield couple's overgrown yard and surprised them by clearing tree limbs and spraying the fence line for free.

stories.headline: Solar Modules Hit Record Efficiency, Holding Strong for the Long Haul
stories.summary: An international team achieved record 22.36% efficiency in solar modules using stabilized hybrid perovskite, maintaining performance after 1,000 hours of operation.
